channel.title: Heap-Space on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Heap-Space on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] Java OutOfMemoryError: Java Heap Space Fix
item.description:
OutOfMemoryError: Java Heap Space

An OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space is thrown when the JVM cannot allocate enough memory for a new object because the heap is full and garbage collection cannot free sufficient space. This is an Error (not an Exception), meaning the JVM considers it unrecoverable.

Description

The JVM heap stores all object instances. When the heap fills up and GC can’t reclaim enough space, the OOME fires. The default heap size varies by JVM but is typically 256MB-1GB.

item.title: Out of Memory: Java Heap Space
item.description:
This error means the Gradle daemon process exceeded its allocated Java heap memory. It typically happens on large projects with many modules or heavy compilation tasks.

Common Causes

  • Default JVM heap size is too small for the project size
  • Memory leak in a custom Gradle plugin or build script
  • Too many concurrent Gradle daemon processes consuming system memory
  • Large generated sources or annotation processing consuming excessive memory

How to Fix

Increase the Gradle daemon JVM memory in gradle.properties:
